data.content:
I can see you have an MSTest project with a failing test — `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` expects `15.00m` but the method returns `10.00m` for express.

Let me start `dotnet watch test` so tests re-run automatically when you save changes:
data.reasoningText:
The user wants to use `dotnet watch test` for hot reload. I can see the project is a .NET 8 MSTest project. The second test (`CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15`) will fail because `CalculateShipping("express", 1.0)` returns `10.00m` but the test expects `15.00m`.

To set up hot reload, I just need to run `dotnet watch test` in the project directory. Let me start that up.

toolCalls.name: bash
toolCalls.args: {"command": "cd /tmp/sv-ec6429312ba1490abc5a9cc012f02941 && dotnet watch test --no-hot-reload 2>&1", "description": "Start dotnet watch test", "mode": "async", "shellId": "watch"}

data.content:
`dotnet watch test` is now running and watching for changes. Here's the current status:

- ✅ `CalculateShipping_StandardDelivery_Returns5` — passed
- ❌ `CalculateShipping_ExpressDelivery_Returns15` — failed (expected `15.00`, got `10.00`)

The watcher will automatically re-run tests whenever you save a file. To fix the failing test, update the `"express"` case in `CalculateShipping` (line 23) from `10.00m` to `15.00m` — or adjust the test expectation if `10.00m` is correct.
